article.title: LWiAI Podcast #246 - Gemini 3.5 + Omni, Musk Loses, OpenAI vs Erdős

harvestedTerms.summary: Podcast episode covering Google I/O highlights (Gemini 3.5, Gemini Spark, Gemini Omni), coding agent competition (Cursor Composer 2.5, Grok Build), business news (Anthropic funding, Musk lawsuit), and research/safety topics including negation neglect, circuit redundancy, autonomous hacking, and AI mathematical proofs.

article.title: How we used Gemini to build Google I/O 2026

harvestedTerms.summary: Google's I/O 2026 behind-the-scenes post describes using Gemini, Nano Banana, Google Antigravity, and other experimental AI tools to produce films, brand identity, and immersive experiences for the event. The article is primarily a marketing/use-case showcase but surfaces several product names worth tracking.

article.title: YouTubers Win the Box Office, Goodbye Gatekeepers, The YouTube Bar

harvestedTerms.summary: Paywalled Stratechery article about YouTubers succeeding at the box office and the erosion of Hollywood gatekeepers. The excerpt is almost entirely subscription/navigation boilerplate — the actual article content is inaccessible. No substantive AI terminology appears in the readable portion. One adjacent reference to 'the Agentic Web' appears in a linked interview title ('An Interview with Parallel Founder Parag Agarwal About Valuing Content on the Agentic Web').

article.title: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked

harvestedTerms.summary: Simon Willison covers a reported security incident where hackers exploited Meta's AI support chatbot to hijack high-profile Instagram accounts by simply asking it to transfer account ownership — no sophisticated prompt injection required. The post highlights a catastrophic AI deployment failure: wiring an agentic support bot to execute irreversible account recovery actions without adequate verification.

longlistAdditions.label: Account Takeover via AI Support Bot

article.title: Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC

harvestedTerms.summary: Anthropic has confidentially submitted a draft S-1 registration statement to the SEC for a proposed IPO of its common stock. The announcement is a brief regulatory notice with no substantive AI terminology or conceptual framing — it references the Series H funding round and mentions Claude product lines in site navigation but introduces no new concepts.
